title: Readonly setting within SQL Backend options should prevent changes to Connections
author: ali-avci
body_snippet:
Beside the requirement that when something is changed in the sql-backend options a restart has to be done. It is also required that none of the configurations might be changed after the read-only flag is set in the options. 

It turned out that a user in a shared jump server had changed some connection configuration data and the tool did save them in the normal .xml file in a faulty way. To prevent such attractions it is needed to lock the complete connection configuration when the read-only f...

iterations.description: The 'Readonly' setting for the SQL backend is not preventing modification operations in the UI, leading to a confusing user experience where unsaved changes are allowed.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: yes that planed to implement with user profiles - then it will be possible to set profile for a user defining what server settings he can update for example and which he just use and not save if any changes are made.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The 'Readonly' setting for the SQL backend is not preventing modification operations in the UI, leading to a confusing user experience where unsaved changes are allowed.
Approach: Modify 'ConnectionTree.cs' to check 'Properties.OptionsDBsPage.Default.SQLReadOnly' before allowing modification operations (add, delete, rename, duplicate, create link). Also, update 'ConnectionContextMenu.cs' to disable relevant menu items when in readonly mode.
